content: Language teaching's lexical reinforcement of the reflection concept can be repurposed as a structured prompt engineering framework to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms, turning passive vocabulary learning of the term 'reflection' into an active scaffolding for agentic self-critique. Just as high school learners are trained to explicitly pause to reflect on their writing process through the lesson structure, LLMs can be fine-tuned to embed this step-by-step reflective habit triggered by the lexical framing of 'reflection' as a defined, actionable task.
